LAW AND MOTION 403, CROSS DEFENDANT PHOENIX AMERICAN INC, RESOURCE/PHOENIX INC, GUS CONSTANTIN, LEASE MANAGEMENT ASSOCIATES INC MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AFTER JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DECISION IN HILLTOP INVESTMENT ASSOCIATES V. LEONE (1994) IS CONTROLLING GIVEN THE FACTS AT ISSUE. IN COMMENCING ARBITRATION, WEST WAS SEEKING TO RECOVER WHAT HE WAS OWED UNDER THE RPC RETENTION AGREEMENT. HE WAS SUCCESSFUL IN DOING SO IN THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDING AND WAS AWARDED ATTORNEYS' FEES FOR DOING SO. PAI AFFIRMATIVELY SOUGHT AND WAS SUCCESSFUL IN NOT HAVING TO PARTICIPATE IN THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDING. IN THE CIVIL LITIGATION BETWEEN WEST AND PAI, THE CLAIMS AGAINST PAI AND THE RELATED PARTIES WERE, IN SUBSTANCE, AN EFFORT ON THE PART OF WEST TO HAVE HIS JUDGMENT FOR BREACH OF THE RETENTION AGREEMENT PAID. THE THRUST OF EVIDENCE PRESENTED AS TO THE PAI PARTIES WAS NOT EVIDENCE DIRECTED TO PROVING A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM BUT CENTERED ON ALLEGED INAPPROPRIATE CONDUCT IN THE TAKEOVER OF RPC BY PAI. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE COURT FINDS THAT IF ANY FEES WERE RECOVERABLE, THE FEES WOULD BE LIMITED TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. AS TO THAT CLAIM, WEST PREVAILED AGAINST RPC AND NOT AGAINST PAI AND THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION UNDER HILLTOP NOT TO AWARD ATTORNEYS' FEES TO PAI. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT PHOENIX AMERICAN INC, RESOURCE/PHOENIX INC, GUS CONSTANTIN, LEASE MANAGEMENT ASSOCIATES INC MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AFTER JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Joseph N. Demko of Jeffer Mangels Butler & Marmaro (398.8080) for moving parties: plaintiff Phoenix American, Inc. and cross-defendants Resource/Phoenix, Inc., Gus Constantin and Lease Management Associates, Inc.
Benjamin Johnson of Quadros & Johnson (650.377.4300) for responding party/defendant W. Corey West

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: denied. The Court finds that the decision in Hilltop Investment Associates v. Leone (1994) is controlling given the facts at issue. In commencing arbitration, West was seeking to recover what he was owed under the RPC retention agreement. He was successful in doing so in the arbitration proceeding and was awarded attorneys' fees for doing so. PAI affirmatively sought and was successful in not having to participate in the arbitration proceeding. In the civil litigation between West and PAI, the claims against PAI and the related parties were, in substance, an effort on the part of West to have his judgment for breach of the Retention Agreement paid. The thrust of evidence presented as to the PAI parties was not evidence directed to proving a breach of contract claim but centered on alleged inappropriate conduct in the takeover of RPC by PAI. As a consequence, the Court finds that if any fees were recoverable, the fees would be limited to the breach of contract claim. As to that claim, West prevailed against RPC and not against PAI and the Court exercises its discretion under Hilltop not to award attorneys' fees to PAI. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (403/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR: 8600
